title: Meta and YouTube Found Negligent in Landmark Social Media Addiction Trial
summary: A jury found the companies harmed a young user with design features that were addictive and led to her mental health distress.

title: The Social Media Addiction Trials: What to Know
summary: Landmark trials are testing a new legal strategy claiming that Meta, TikTok, Snap and YouTube caused personal injury through addictive products.

title: Meta Lays Off 700 Employees, While Rewarding Top Executives
summary: The jobs cuts and a new stock program for executives come as Meta continues to shift its focus to artificial intelligence.

title: Trump Draws Bipartisan Backlash for Easing Oil Sanctions on Russia and Iran
summary: Republicans and Democrats alike have criticized the Trump administration’s moves, taken to stabilize oil markets rocked by the war with Iran, warning that it is benefiting two U.S. adversaries.

title: Asia Is Getting Crushed Between Oil Prices and the Dollar
summary: From India to Southeast Asia to South Korea, currencies are crumbling as governments race to secure fuel that is priced in American money.

title: Trump Says He’s Talking With Iran. Iran Says He’s Not. Here’s Why.
summary: The president has domestic and international political motivations for touting negotiations to end the war. Iran has similar ones to deny discussions.

title: ICE Agents at Some Airports Begin Checking IDs in Security Lines
summary: It was unclear whether having agents helping with screening passengers would improve wait times. Some travelers expressed worries.

title: Why Are Delays at Houston’s Bush Airport Worse Than at Others?
summary: Lines were long at airports nationwide Tuesday amid T.S.A. staffing shortages, but passengers at Bush Intercontinental Airport waited more than four hours to pass through checkpoints.

title: Air Canada C.E.O. Draws Scorn for Delivering Condolences Only in English
summary: The lack of French in Michael Rousseau’s speech about the deadly collision at LaGuardia Airport reignited a debate over linguistic inclusivity in Canada.

title: Melania Trump Appears With a Robot, Saying More Children Should Be Educated by Them
summary: The first lady, Melania Trump, believes that more children should be educated by “humanoid educators.”

title: Trump to Delay Nominating New C.D.C. Director
summary: The administration has yet to find a candidate who aligns with Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s agenda while avoiding his unpopular stance on vaccines.

title: Kate Marvel, Prominent Climate Scientist, Resigns From NASA
summary: Kate Marvel, a well-known author, joins an estimated 95,000 people who have left federal science agencies since President Trump returned to the White House.

title: Senate Housing Bill Sparks Debate About Who Gets to Own Single-Family Homes
summary: Within the Senate’s housing bill lie the terms of an unusual debate: Who gets to own — and live in — single-family homes?

title: For 2nd Time, an Appeals Court Backs the Trump Administration’s Detention Policy
summary: Courts are weighing whether the administration can hold undocumented immigrants without bond, an issue that may be resolved by the Supreme Court.

title: Heirs of Dave the Potter, the Enslaved Artist, Are Battling to Recover His Legacy
summary: The descendants of David Drake learned who he was 10 years ago. They see his jars as his artistic and spiritual inheritance — and their own.

title: A Phone-Free Childhood? One Irish Village Is Making It Happen.
summary: Tired of seeing its elementary-school children struggle with online temptations, the town of Greystones proposed a ‘no smart devices’ code. Most everyone bought in.

title: James Talarico Responds With ‘Love’ To Hegseth Pastor’s Criticism
summary: James Talarico, the Democratic nominee for Senate in Texas, said, “You may pray for my death, Pastor, but I still love you.” The pastor said he was calling for Mr. Talarico’s religious conversion.

title: With Their Voter Bill Stymied, G.O.P. Leaders Ponder a Plan B
summary: Republicans are eying a last-ditch procedural maneuver to overcome united Democratic opposition, but the chances for success are slim.

title: What to Know About Democrat Emily Gregory’s Win in Florida
summary: A mother of three won a statehouse seat that includes Mar-a-Lago, while a union electrician leads in a State Senate race in conservative West Tampa.

title: Democrats Have Fundraising Edge in Virginia Redistricting Battle Ahead of April Referendum
summary: Republicans are cautiously optimistic about a statewide referendum now at the center of the country’s gerrymandering war, but Democrats have a huge cash advantage.

title: How Bald Reddit Helps People Manage Hair Loss
summary: A Reddit forum has become a haven for those deciding whether it’s time to shave it all.

title: Leon Radvinsky, 43, Dies; Built the Adult-Entertainment Giant OnlyFans
summary: By leveraging social media and the influencer economy, he turned his website into a byword for online pornography in the 21st century.

title: Mozart Wouldn’t Be Mozart Without These Three Objects
summary: Hear the instruments and scores, on view in a new exhibition at the Morgan Library and Museum, that proved foundational for Mozart’s life in music.

title: This Should Be Trump’s Next Move With Iran
summary: An oil blockade could pressure Iran. Sanctions relief would do the opposite.

summary: In one swoop, it overturned the conditions for conciliation.

summary: Also, Iran signals an openness to negotiations. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

title: Judge Orders Arrest of Matt Bevin, Former Kentucky Governor, for Contempt
summary: Seven years after leaving office, Matt Bevin, the combative ex-governor, faces charges for not providing financial records sought by his estranged son.

title: Tracy Kidder, Author of ‘The Soul of a New Machine,’ Dies at 80
summary: A Pulitzer Prize-winning narrative journalist, he wrote deeply reported books that often focused on heroic goodness in people.

title: Canada’s Supreme Court Hears Case on Ability to Suspend Constitutional Rights
summary: Quebec’s ban on religious symbols — and a measure that suspends constitutional rights — are being tested in a case with far-reaching repercussions.

title: Day Care Worker Stole Millions for Trips and W.W.E. Tickets, U.S. Says
summary: Murielle Misczak was arrested Wednesday morning and is accused of using the money on wrestling tickets, luxury vacations and food deliveries.
